items.title: Cache stable prompt prefixes to cut LLM cost and latency
items.why: Marking the unchanging part of a prompt (system instructions, tool defs, RAG/codebase context) as cacheable makes repeat calls reuse it at ~10% of input price and skip prefix compute, so cost and time-to-first-token drop sharply.
how_to_steps: Identify the stable prefix (system prompt, tool defs, long context) that repeats across calls and ensure it meets the model minimum (~1,024 tokens).
Add `cache_control: {type: 'ephemeral'}` to those blocks; keep the prefix byte-identical (no injected timestamps, no reordered tools).
Log `cache_read_input_tokens` and `cache_creation_input_tokens` from the response usage to verify hit rates.
For batch/agentic runs, fire calls within the TTL (5-min default or the 1-hour option) and pre-warm the cache before user traffic.
items.evidence: Anthropic reports up to 90% lower input cost (cache reads at 0.1x) and up to 85% lower time-to-first-token; documented cases cut a bill from $720 -> $72/mo and per-page generation from $0.42 -> $0.06 (86%).
items.try_today: Add cache_control to the system prompt of one high-volume call and check cache_read_input_tokens on the second request.
